commands.name: exe
commands.description: Windows self-extracting installer (.exe). If --stub is not provided, the tool downloads the appropriate stub from GitHub Releases.

options.name: --stub
options.description: Path to the prebuilt stub (WinExe) to concatenate (optional if repo layout is present)

options.name: --setup-logo
options.description: Path to a logo image displayed by the installer and uninstaller wizards

options.name: --arch
options.description: Target architecture for the stub (x64, arm64)

commands.name: from-project
commands.description: Publish a .NET project and build a Windows self-extracting installer (.exe). If --stub is not provided, the tool downloads the appropriate stub from GitHub Releases.

options.name: --stub
options.description: Path to the prebuilt stub (WinExe) to concatenate (optional if repo layout is present)

options.name: --setup-logo
options.description: Path to a logo image displayed by the installer and uninstaller wizards

commands.name: from-project
commands.description: Publish a .NET project and build an MSIX from the published output (expects manifest/assets).

commands.name: pack
commands.description: Create an MSIX from a directory (expects AppxManifest.xml in the tree or pre-baked metadata). Experimental.

commands.name: pack-appimage
commands.description: Create a portable AppImage (.AppImage) from a published directory. Use subcommands for AppDir workflows.

options.name: --output
options.description: Destination path for the generated .AppImage file

options.name: --summary
options.description: Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.

options.name: --appId
options.description: Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication

options.name: --is-terminal
options.description: Indicates whether your application is a terminal application

commands.name: appdir
commands.description: Creates an AppDir from a directory (does not package an .AppImage). For .NET apps, pass the publish directory.

options.name: --summary
options.description: Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.

options.name: --appId
options.description: Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication

options.name: --is-terminal
options.description: Indicates whether your application is a terminal application

commands.name: from-appdir
commands.description: Creates an AppImage from an existing AppDir directory.

options.name: --executable-relative-path
options.description: Executable inside the AppDir (relative), e.g., usr/bin/MyApp

options.name: --summary
options.description: Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.

options.name: --appId
options.description: Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication

options.name: --is-terminal
options.description: Indicates whether your application is a terminal application

commands.name: from-project
commands.description: Publish a .NET project and build an AppImage from the published output.

options.name: --arch
options.description: Target architecture (x64, arm64). Auto-detects from current system if not specified.

options.name: --summary
options.description: Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.

options.name: --appId
options.description: Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication

options.name: --is-terminal
options.description: Indicates whether your application is a terminal application

commands.name: pack-deb
commands.description: Create a Debian (.deb) installer for Debian and Ubuntu based distributions.

options.name: --output
options.description: Destination path for the generated .deb file

options.name: --summary
options.description: Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.

options.name: --appId
options.description: Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication

options.name: --is-terminal
options.description: Indicates whether your application is a terminal application

commands.name: from-project
commands.description: Publish a .NET project and build a Debian .deb from the published output.

options.name: --arch
options.description: Target architecture (x64, arm64). Auto-detects from current system if not specified.

options.name: --summary
options.description: Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.

options.name: --appId
options.description: Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication

options.name: --is-terminal
options.description: Indicates whether your application is a terminal application

commands.name: pack-dmg
commands.description: Create a macOS disk image (.dmg) with a native HFS+ payload wrapped in UDIF (DMG) format.

options.name: --output
options.description: Destination path for the generated .dmg file

options.name: --summary
options.description: Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.

options.name: --appId
options.description: Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication

options.name: --is-terminal
options.description: Indicates whether your application is a terminal application

options.name: --with-default-layout
options.description: Add a default Finder layout (background image, Applications link positioning) when none is provided

commands.name: from-project
commands.description: Publish a .NET project and build a .dmg from the published output (.app bundle auto-generated if missing). Experimental.

options.name: --with-default-layout
options.description: Add a default Finder layout (background image, Applications link positioning) when none is provided

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Verify that a .dmg file has a macOS-friendly structure (ISO/UDTO or UDIF).

commands.name: pack-rpm
commands.description: Create an RPM (.rpm) package suitable for Fedora, openSUSE, and other RPM-based distributions.

options.name: --output
options.description: Destination path for the generated .rpm file

options.name: --summary
options.description: Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.

options.name: --appId
options.description: Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication

options.name: --is-terminal
options.description: Indicates whether your application is a terminal application

commands.name: from-project
commands.description: Publish a .NET project and build an RPM from the published output (no code duplication; library drives the pipeline).

options.name: --arch
options.description: Target architecture (x64, arm64). Auto-detects from current system if not specified.

options.name: --summary
options.description: Summary. Short description that should not end in a dot.

options.name: --appId
options.description: Application Id. Usually a Reverse DNS name like com.SomeCompany.SomeApplication

options.name: --is-terminal
options.description: Indicates whether your application is a terminal application
